case_metadata.case_name: Roscoe v. Secretary of Health and Human Services
case_metadata.court: United States Court of Federal Claims

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Petitioner alleges that as a result of receiving hepatitis A (“Hep A”), tetanus-diphtheria-acellular pertussis (“Tdap”), meningococcal (“Menactra”), and human papillomavirus (“HPV”) vaccines on March 31, 2009, B.R. suffered fever, leg and bodily pain, emotional and mental confusion and anguish, and death.
evidence_index.summary: Petitioner claims vaccines caused B.R.'s symptoms and death.
evidence_index.speaker: Petitioner

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: The undersigned finds that petitioner provided preponderant evidence that one or more of the vaccines B.R. received caused her to develop a Streptococcus pyogenes infection that caused her death, which satisfies her burden of proof under Althen v. Secretary of Health & Human Services.
evidence_index.summary: Court finds vaccines caused infection leading to death.
evidence_index.speaker: Special Master

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Dr. Livingston conducted a physical examination, noting that B.R.’s oropharynx was “mildly injected.”
evidence_index.summary: Dr. Livingston noted mild injection in B.R.'s oropharynx.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Livingston

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: B.R. was born on September 4, 1997. Pet. Ex. 14 at 1. Prior to the March 31, 2009 vaccinations, she had no prior significant illnesses, no previous surgeries, and no hospitalizations since birth.
evidence_index.summary: B.R. had no significant medical history before vaccinations.
evidence_index.speaker: Medical Record

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: After B.R. passed away, Ms. Roscoe and Mr. Parks wanted to know what caused B.R.’s death and decided to pay for a full autopsy to be performed.
evidence_index.summary: Family requested a full autopsy to determine cause of death.
evidence_index.speaker: Family

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Dr. Mills submitted an expert affidavit, in which he testified that the nurses at Phoebe Putney violated the standard of care by failing to notify a physician on April 4, 2009 of B.R.’s vital signs at 12:00 midnight (BP 76/52, heart rate 140, and respiratory rate 36) and at 4:00 AM (BP 90/48, heart rate 140, and respiratory rate 32).
evidence_index.summary: Dr. Mills testified nurses failed to notify physician of critical vitals.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Mills

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: The undersigned finds petitioner has provided preponderant evidence of a proximate temporal relationship between B.R.’s vaccinations and the first manifestation of the infection which led to her death.
evidence_index.summary: Court finds temporal relationship between vaccination and infection.
evidence_index.speaker: Special Master

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: The undersigned cannot ignore the fact of B.R.’s vaccinations, and the resulting portal of entry that each injection created, so as to entertain a suggestion that they did not play a role, and thus conclude that the cause here was idiopathic.
evidence_index.summary: Court rejects idiopathic cause, acknowledges vaccine as entry point.
evidence_index.speaker: Special Master

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: B.R. progressively worsened and despite lengthy resuscitative efforts, she died at 12:27 PM on April 4, 2009.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Dr. Latham also concluded that necrotizing fasciitis due to gram positive cocci of the left upper leg, hip, and inguinal/groin area with septic shock was the cause of death.
evidence_index.summary: Dr. Latham concluded necrotizing fasciitis and septic shock caused death.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Latham

verdict_detected.key_phrase: petitioner has established by preponderant evidence that she is entitled to compensation.

initial_state.chief_complaint: Fever, leg and bodily pain, emotional and mental confusion
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Symptoms began after receiving multiple vaccines on March 31, 2009.
initial_state.past_medical_history: No significant medical history before vaccinations

vital_signs.blood_pressure: BP 76/52 at midnight, BP 90/48 at 4:00 AM

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient with severe hypotension and tachycardia post-vaccination
decision_points.prompt: What should be the immediate management step?

options.clinical_reasoning: Critical vital signs require immediate physician intervention
Failure to recognize severity of vital signs

actual_action_defendant.description: Nurses did not notify physician of critical vitals

expected_action_court.description: Physician should have been notified immediately
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert testimony indicated standard of care was breached

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to notify physician of critical vitals

decision_points.prompt: What diagnostic test should be ordered?

expected_action_court.description: Blood cultures should have been ordered

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to order necessary diagnostic tests
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Blood cultures should have been ordered

timeline_phases.name: Clinical Deterioration
timeline_phases.description: Patient's condition worsens with critical vital signs

patient_outcome.description: Patient developed necrotizing fasciitis and septic shock, leading to death

key_findings: Breach of duty in failing to notify physician of critical vitals
Causation established between vaccination and infection

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to notify physician and order necessary tests
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Timely intervention could have prevented deterioration

allegations.allegation: Failure to notify physician of critical vitals

court_findings.finding: Vaccines caused infection leading to death
